description: Machine-readable canonical vocabulary for the context engineering discipline and Enterprise Context Fabric architecture.

concepts.name: Context Engineering
concepts.definition: The discipline responsible for designing how contextual information is assembled, structured, and delivered to AI systems and workflows.

concepts.name: Enterprise Context Fabric
concepts.definition: A conceptual architecture responsible for assembling and delivering contextual information across enterprise systems for AI workflows and decision processes.

concepts.name: Enterprise AI Memory
concepts.definition: The continuity of enterprise knowledge across time, interactions, systems, and workflows that allows AI systems to operate with awareness of historical context.

concepts.name: Time-to-Context
concepts.definition: A conceptual metric representing how quickly relevant context can be assembled and delivered to a task, decision, or AI system.

concepts.name: Context Ingestion
concepts.definition: The process of collecting contextual signals from enterprise systems.

concepts.name: Context Assembly
concepts.definition: The process of connecting contextual signals across systems to form a coherent contextual view.

concepts.name: Context Structuring
concepts.definition: The process of organizing contextual information into structured representations suitable for AI consumption.

concepts.name: Context Delivery
concepts.definition: The process of providing relevant context to AI systems, workflows, or user interfaces.

concepts.name: Context Capsule
concepts.definition: A structured package of contextual information assembled for a specific task, role, workflow, or decision.

concepts.name: Enterprise Signal
concepts.definition: An event, state change, activity, or artifact generated by enterprise systems.

concepts.name: Structured Context
concepts.definition: Organized, classified information assembled from enterprise signals and ready for AI consumption.

concepts.name: Context Relationship
concepts.definition: A logical connection between contextual entities, events, or artifacts that helps explain how elements of enterprise activity relate to each other.

concepts.name: Context Infrastructure
concepts.definition: Infrastructure responsible for assembling and delivering contextual information across enterprise systems.

concepts.name: Context Fabric Layer
concepts.definition: An individual architectural layer within an Enterprise Context Fabric, responsible for a specific stage of the context lifecycle.

concepts.name: Context Delivery Interface
concepts.definition: An interface through which structured context is delivered to AI systems or workflows.

concepts.name: Assembly Pattern
concepts.definition: A pre-defined specification that describes what signals to gather, how to structure them, and what governance rules to apply during context assembly.

concepts.name: Deterministic Context Assembly
concepts.definition: The practice of assembling context using pre-defined, repeatable patterns rather than probabilistic retrieval methods.

concepts.name: Context Governance
concepts.definition: The application of access controls, compliance rules, data classification, and audit trails to context throughout its lifecycle.

concepts.name: Enterprise Context Bottleneck
concepts.definition: The gap between the contextual information an AI system needs to perform effectively and the contextual information it actually receives.

concepts.name: Freshness Window
concepts.definition: The time period during which assembled context is considered current and valid for delivery without reassembly.

concepts.name: Source System
concepts.definition: An enterprise platform from which signals are extracted during context ingestion.

concepts.name: ContextECF
concepts.definition: A platform designed to implement the principles of Enterprise Context Fabric architecture, focusing on reducing Time-to-Context for enterprise AI systems.

concepts.name: Context Window Budget
concepts.definition: The allocation of AI model context window capacity across different types of input — instructions, retrieved documents, assembled context, and conversation history.

concepts.name: Context Composition
concepts.definition: The process of combining multiple Context Capsules into a unified context delivery for complex, multi-domain tasks.

concepts.name: Context Provenance Chain
concepts.definition: The full traceability path of a signal from its origin in a source system through ingestion, assembly, structuring, and delivery to an AI consumer.

concepts.name: Context Freshness Score
concepts.definition: A quantified measure of how current assembled context is relative to the latest state of its contributing source systems.

concepts.name: Agentic Context Protocol
concepts.definition: A standardized interface pattern for autonomous AI agents to request and receive incremental context during multi-step task execution.

concepts.name: Cross-Tenant Context Isolation
concepts.definition: Governance mechanisms ensuring that context assembled for one organizational tenant cannot be accessed by or leaked to another tenant within a shared context infrastructure deployment.

concepts.name: Context Replay
concepts.definition: The ability to reconstruct the exact context that was delivered for a past interaction, supporting audit, debugging, and compliance review.
